results.owner_or_reason: ENG-2717, ENG-2718, ENG-2719, ENG-2728, extension-corpus, extsweep
ENG-2713, ENG-2717, ENG-2722, ENG-2723, ENG-2724, extension-corpus, extsweep
E1-S1 (MV3 scaffold) implements AD-5a — 'mechanism now RESOLVED = Chrome MV3 browser extension' (ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.md AD-5a); FR-PA2 is that same resolved-mechanism requirement under the AD-5a alias

E2-S1 'Guided one-step connect ... <=3 taps <=2 min, zero config' + E6-S4 'FR-O4 first-run usability test — connect <=3 taps <=2 min, no config, verified' — PRD text itself says FR-CN1 '(Folds FR-O4)' and FR-D2's acceptance is FR-CN1, both covered under the FR-D2/FR-O4 alias
E2-S3 'Per-assistant connection-status surface + honest not-yet-supported messaging' (covers FR-D2, FR-D3) is verbatim FR-CN2's content (truthful per-assistant status), under the FR-D2/FR-D3 alias

no story asserts 'no Orvex-held provider credentials / no OAuth to the provider'; E2-S1/E2-S2 build the Orvex-side delegated-principal mint+storage but never test/claim the provider-connection step avoids OAuth or credential collection — grepped all extension bodies, no hit

no extension story builds a designed empty/again state on a connection surface; PRD flags it '[ASSUMPTION — aligns with FR-O3; UI detail owned by ui service]' but the only FR-O3 story found (ui/E3-GAP1, gap-hunt/fix-bodies/fix-fr-o3-empty-states.md) is scoped to memory surfaces (wall/queue/history/coach), not extension connection surfaces — grepped extension bodies for 'empty state', zero hits

no story treats retrieved memory as untrusted data vs instructions (stored-prompt-injection defense); E1-S3/E4-S3 'untrusted' hits are about the third-party PROVIDER PAGE being untrusted, a different claim — grepped for 'untrusted'+memory / 'not instructions', zero hits on the CF4 claim

no story implements per-assistant disconnect or standing-consent revocation with fail-closed semantics; E2-S2's 'revoke' is the extension's own Orvex auth-token revoke (global), not a per-assistant disconnect or a per-use/private-memory consent revocation — E4-S1 (the consent-gate story) has zero revoke/disconnect content

E1-S3 'host_permissions lists only the beachhead provider origins and no <all_urls>' + E6-S1 AC1 'justified minimal permissions' store-review lint — together are FR-TS1's minimum-host-permissions + store-listing-justification requirement

E4-S3 AC1 'only page read is via the compose accessor — no full-page text' + AC2 'only outbound host is Orvex API; provider page content never posted anywhere' is FR-TS3's no-read/no-transmit-beyond-confirming-own-write requirement verbatim

cited explicitly as a PRD source in E1-S3 AC2/refs, E3-S1 AC2, E4-S1 refs, E4.md, E4-S3 AC2/refs, E6-S2 AC4/refs — default-off background recall / user-initiated delivery is asserted across six extension stories

grepped every extension story body for 'neutral'/'lock-in'/'portable' — zero hits; the multi-provider adapter framework (E3-S1) delivers cross-assistant reach but no story or test asserts 'no lock-in' / 'memory stays portable' as its own claim
cited explicitly in E1-S1 AC4, E2-S1 AC4, E5-S1 AC4/refs, E5-S2 refs, E5-S3 refs, E5.md, E6-S1 AC3 — graceful degradation / never-white-screen is asserted across seven extension stories
E6-S3 AC1-AC3 (a11y zero-critical, dual-theme, keyboard nav) + E6.md, explicitly cited as NFR-5 non-technical accessibility
